summary: Epic Other One suite with soaring jams and tight rhythm section
blurb: The second set delivers a monster The Other One marathon weaving through Cryptical Envelopment, Me & My Uncle and a blistering Not Fade Away > Goin' Down the Road Feeling Bad sequence. Jerry’s guitar is in the zone while Phil drives the jam with bass wizardry, and Keith’s organ shines front‑and‑center. It ranks among the band’s 1971 peaks.
review: December 1, 1971 at Boston Music Hall finds the Dead firing on all cylinders, especially in an explosive second set that has become a benchmark for the era. The night kicks off with a tight‑knit jam through Cryptical Envelopment into a marathon The Other One, punctuated by a scorching Me & My Uncle and a double Not Fade Away/Going Down the Road Feeling Bad finale that keeps the energy soaring. Jerry is described as “in the zone” – his call‑and‑response on The Other One is “smoking fire,” delivering supersonic solos that cut through the mix. Phil turns into a “bass ninja,” laying down a monster groove that drives the entire suite, while Keith’s organ steps out of the background, taking a front‑and‑center role that many reviewers call his best performance. The drum segment, highlighted for its cohesion and power, adds the perfect rhythmic backbone, and even though Bob’s rhythm guitar can be buried in some mixes, he still locks into the collective push. Across all sources the show is praised as one of the band’s 1971 high points, with a soundboard capture that preserves every nuance of this historic jam‑fest.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four of five recordings are overwhelmingly positive, repeatedly calling the performance 'monstrous', 'in the zone', and praising the Other One suite, Cryptical Envelopment, and Keith's organ. The lone mixed review only comments on audience tape quality, not on musical flaws. This strong consensus yields a high positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Superb soundboard capture with crisp, balanced audio that showcases the epic Other One suite and Keith’s organ without major defects.

key_highlights: Explosive Other One marathon weaving through Cryptical Envelopment and Me & My Uncle
High‑energy Not Fade Away > Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad sequence
Keith Godchaux’s organ front‑and‑center, highlighted as possibly his best performance
Phil Lesh’s bass drives the jam with monster intensity
song_highlights: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad

band_performance.Jerry: In the zone with a supersonic, 'smoking fire' guitar tone; call‑and‑response on The Other One is amazing.
band_performance.Phil: Bass ninja who drives the extended jams, described as monster and wickedly into the music.
band_performance.Bob: Rhythm guitar sometimes buried in the mix but contributes to the tight lock‑step performance.
band_performance.Brent: Keith’s organ shines front‑and‑center, providing fresh texture and highlighted as a standout moment throughout the suite.
band_performance.Mickey: Drum segment praised for energy and cohesion, essential to the era’s sound.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
